title: ipomoea kerinciensis
intro: Ipomoea kerinciensis, also known as the Kerinci morning glory, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It is native to the Kerinci Seblat National Park in Sumatra, Indonesia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. In traditional herbal preparations, Ipomoea kerinciensis is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, made by steeping the plant's leaves in hot water, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
